//: Third-party forks: copy this file to giscus.local.json in the repo root, then replace the values below with your OWN giscus comments repo + category IDs. giscus.local.json is gitignored, so your settings never collide with an upstream merge, and you don't have to edit src/_data/giscus.js (engine code) or run the fetch script that patches it. Once present it becomes this fork's default comment board on every build path - npm start, GitHub Pages, and any cPanel deploy that doesn't select a GISCUS_PROFILE. Delete this '//' key in your copy (or leave it - JSON has no comments, and giscus.js ignores unknown keys). Get the repo + four category IDs the same way as the main repo: create a public comments repo, enable Discussions, create the categories, install the giscus app, then run `GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxx node scripts/fetch-giscus-ids.js --repo your-org/your-comments-repo` (prints the five values) - see README.md's 'Discussion forum (giscus)' section.
